LAW AND MOTION 302, RE: PLAINTIFF'S MOTION TO COMPEL BINDING ARBITRATION AND TO STAY THE INSTANT ACTION. NO APPEARANCE WAS MADE BY ANY PARTY, COUNSEL, OR INTERESTED PERSON. THE COURT ADOPTS ITS TENTATIVE RULING AS FOLLOWS: OFF CALENDAR. THE CASE IS STAYED AS TO DEFENDANT SHIFT OPERATIONS, LLC PER A FILING IN THE UNITED STATES BANKRUPTCY COURT FOR THE NORTHERN DISTRICT OF CALIFORNIA. JUDGE: RICHARD B. ULMER. CLERK: ROBERT WOODS. NOT REPORTED. (302/RBU)

Notice Of Motion And Motion To Compel Binding Arbitration And To Stay The Instant Action
Off Calendar Nov-01-2023 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:

RE: Plaintiff's Motion To Compel Binding Arbitration And To Stay The Instant Action.

No appearance was made by any Party, Counsel, or Interested Person.

The court adopts its tentative ruling as follows: Off calendar. The case is stayed as to defendant Shift Operations, LLC per a filing in the United States Bankruptcy Court for the Northern District of California.

Judge: Richard B. Ulmer. Clerk: Robert Woods. Not Reported. (302/RBU)
